originalAlarm.description: `curr` could be null (null value originating from line 65) and is dereferenced.

model:
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_RECURSIVE () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES () Bool
  true)
reasoning: Line 66: `curr->val` is dereferenced immediately after malloc at line 65 without a NULL check. If malloc returns NULL, this is a null dereference. The bug is in main() outside any #ifdef block, so it occurs in ALL configurations. No '// ERROR' comment at this location, so this is a new bug not documented in VBDB. The PC (RECURSIVE=T AND SORTFILES=T) is over-constrained — neither flag affects the malloc/dereference in main(). Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
